steps.title: Match the lasting memory clue
steps.content: The passage contrasts Hale's accomplishments with the lasting historical recognition gained by being first. The example of Aloha Wanderwell shows that people remember a first achievement. The blank should say that little can surpass being first when gaining a lasting place in historical memory.

steps.title: Reject unrelated actions
steps.content: A means exceed proper limits, not surpass in importance. B means change unpredictably with something, which does not fit lasting historical memory. C means restrict within limits, the opposite of the intended idea that being first is hard to beat. The answer is D.
